title: Can a Town of Culture Revive British Towns?
subtitle: 小镇文化奖争议：称号能否带来长期复兴

paragraphs.en: A group of Labour MPs, including Yvette Cooper, began the year by proposing a UK “town of culture” award. They want it to sit alongside the existing city of culture title, which was held by Hull in 2017 and awarded to Coventry for 2021. They argue that the benefits seen in Hull should not be limited to big cities.
paragraphs.zh: 新年伊始，包括伊薇特·库珀在内的一批工党议员提出设立英国“文化小镇”奖。他们希望该奖项与现有的“文化之城”称号并行，后者曾在2017年授予赫尔，并已授予2021年的考文垂。他们认为，赫尔获得的正面效应不应只属于大城市。

paragraphs.en: Hull's title brought in about £220 million of investment and a flood of arts activity. British towns are not barred from applying for major culture titles, but they often lack the resources to outbid bigger rivals. A dedicated town award could become an annual program that attracts funding and jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 赫尔获评后吸引了约2.2亿英镑投资，并带来大量艺术活动。英国小镇并非不能申请重要文化称号，但它们通常缺乏与大城市竞争所需的申报资源。设立专门的“文化小镇”奖，有望形成年度机制，持续吸引资金并创造就业。

words.meaning: v. 出价超过，竞标胜过

paragraphs.en: Critics call the idea a consolation prize now that Britain can no longer compete for the more prestigious European Capital of Culture title. Glasgow won that title in 1990 and Liverpool in 2008. Cynics even joke that the next label might be village of culture or suburb of culture.
paragraphs.zh: 批评者认为，这一提案只是英国无法再申请更高含金量“欧洲文化之都”后的安慰奖。格拉斯哥和利物浦曾分别在1990年与2008年获得这一称号。悲观者甚至讽刺说，接下来也许会出现“文化村”或“文化郊区”之类的新标签。

words.meaning: adj. 有声望的，享有盛誉的

paragraphs.en: Still, titles are not a cure-all. A poorly run “year of culture” can wash through a place like the tide, creating brief publicity but no lasting gains. Truly successful hosts do more than fill hotels or stage one season of headline events.
paragraphs.zh: 不过，任何头衔都不是万灵药。一个执行不善的“文化年”会像潮水一样迅速退去，只留下短暂曝光，却没有长期收益。真正成功的地区远不止于提高酒店入住率或举办一季高规格活动。

paragraphs.en: They raise local ambition and shift civic self-image toward confidence and optimism. Doing that requires unusual vision, plus sustained cooperation among local authorities, businesses, community groups, and cultural organizations. It is difficult, but it is possible.
paragraphs.zh: 它们能够提升当地居民的志向，并把城市自我认知推向更自信、更乐观的方向。要做到这一点，需要很强的愿景，也需要政府、企业、社区与文化机构长期协作。这件事很难，但并非做不到。

paragraphs.en: Glasgow in the 1990s is a useful example: the title did not create change from nothing, but it accelerated change already underway. We still do not know what yearly culture awards can achieve if pursued seriously over time. But if the policy helps even a few British towns regain pride and direction, it will be worth trying.
paragraphs.zh: 格拉斯哥在20世纪90年代的经验说明，头衔本身并不会凭空制造转型，但能加速已经开始的改变。我们仍不确定，若长期认真推进，年度文化奖究竟能发挥多大作用。但只要它能帮助哪怕少数英国小镇重建自豪感与方向感，这项政策就值得尝试。
